nameMeanings.name: Gunter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German (mainly Günter) and English: from the ancient Germanic personal name Gunter (Old French Gontier), composed of the elements gund ‘battle’ + hari, heri ‘army’. Compare Guenter .

variants.name: Gunterman
meanings: Americanized form of German (North Rhine-Westphalia) Guntermann: elaborated form of Gunter . Compare Counterman .

variants.name: Gonter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhardt, Frieda.  Jewish Avrohom.
German: from a variant of the personal name Günter (see Gunter ) or an altered form of Ganter .
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): occupational name, a Yiddishized form of Ukrainian gontar ‘shingle maker’.

variants.name: Gonthier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Henri, Alexandre, Andre, Chantale, Denys, Gilles, Monique, Normand, Piere, Renald.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Gundhari, composed of the elements gund ‘battle’ + hari, heri ‘army’ (see Gunter ). Compare Gauthier 2.
History: Bernard Gonthier from Paris, France, married Marguerite Paquet in Quebec City, QC, in 1676.

variants.name: Gunsch
meanings: German (Günsch): from a short form of the personal name Günther (see Gunter ).

variants.name: Gunther
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Gerhard, Fritz, Otto, Rudi, Alphons, Arno, Bernd, Dieter, Ehrhardt, Erwin, Gunther.

variants.name: Goenner
meanings: North German (Gönner): from the personal name Gönner, a variant of Gunter , composed of the elements gund ‘battle’ + hari, heri ‘army’. Compare Gonnerman .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard Gunter was born in Wales. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in Dexter, Lane, Oregon, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Gunter est né à Galles. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé à Dexter, Lane, Oregon, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Gunter wurde in Wales geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb in Dexter, Lane, Oregon, Vereinigte Staaten.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Gunter(은)는 웨일스에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 미국 오리건 주 랜 데스터에서 사망했다.
